name: General GPP
description: Balanced general physical preparedness across strength, conditioning, mobility, and work capacity. No single quality dominates.

phase_sequence.focus: Foundation — linear barbell 3×/week, Z2 aerobic, KB technique

notes: Best starting goal for athletes transitioning from sport-specific to GPP.

name: Alpine Climbing
description: Aerobic engine dominates. High volume Z2 and rucking, with strength as a supporting quality for summit days.

phase_sequence.focus: Z1/Z2 volume, 3×5 strength, mobility, joint prep
Aerobic volume + vertical, pack weight rucks, thresholds

notes: Polarized 80/20 is the governing framework. Avoid high-intensity strength during aerobic blocks.

name: SOF Operator
description: Durability and work capacity first. Prepare for selection and sustainment of operational fitness: rucking, SSST, PT standards.

phase_sequence.focus: 3×5 linear, Z2 runs, KB ballistics, joint prep
Sandbag complexes, loaded carries, EMOM, ruck extension
PT test standards, SSST prep, named Horsemen WODs

notes: SOF fitness is durability over peak. High-rep, high-frequency, sub-maximal efforts.

name: BJJ Competitor
description: Mat fitness is non-transferable. Supplement rolling with strength endurance, grip conditioning, and aerobic base.

phase_sequence.focus: Aerobic base, hip mobility, grip conditioning, rolling volume
Strength endurance, positional drilling, live rounds
Competition simulation, reduced strength work, sharpening

notes: Rolling frequency is sacred — never reduce mat time for S&C gains.

name: Ultra Endurance
description: Aerobic base is the entire program. Strength is maintenance only. Maximum time on feet, fat adaptation, zone-2 dominance.

phase_sequence.focus: Zone 1/2 volume, fat adaptation, strength maintenance

notes: Interference with strength is real. Keep strength work minimal and separatedin time.

name: Max Strength
description: Barbell dominates. Linear or block periodization. Minimal conditioning interference. For novice-to-intermediate strength development.
notes: Concurrent aerobic work at >20% of training time impairs strength adaptation.

name: Injury Rehab
description: Injury is a phase, not a break. Structured return to training with clinical constraints respected.

phase_sequence.focus: Progressive loading, tissue adaptation, aerobic maintenance
Return to sport — gradual load reintroduction

notes: All loads RPE-based. Pain is always a hard stop signal.
